articles.title: The Isak saga which damaged two seasons
articles.summary: Alexander Isak's move from Newcastle United to Liverpool was the transfer saga of last summer. BBC Sport looks at how it ended up affecting both clubs.

articles.title: From non-league to Premier League... Coventry's 'unreal' pair
articles.summary: Sky Blues forwards Brandon Thomas-Asante and Ephron Mason-Clark have shared a similar path to the top flight.

articles.title: Shankland contract gamble paid off for Hearts - Jones
articles.summary: Lawrence Shankland left Hearts to join Rangers within days of their runners-up finish in the Scottish Premiership.

articles.title: How do Aston Villa improve after making history?
articles.summary: After Europa League success capped their best season for decades, how will Aston Villa move to the next level?

articles.title: Sabalenka calls for more women's matches in French Open night sessions
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka hopes French Open organisers have been persuaded to put more women’s matches on in the night session after her high-class performance against Naomi Osaka on Monday.

articles.title: Pink balls to be trialled in day Tests to maximise playing time
articles.summary: Test-playing nations will be able to ‌use pink balls instead of red ones in traditional daytime matches ‌as part of a trial to prevent play being ​lost due to bad light.

articles.title: Arsenal eye Kroupi & Rogers - and a new Arteta deal
articles.summary: After their long-awaited Premier League title triumph, senior football correspondent Sami Mokbel assesses Arsenal's to-do list for this summer.

articles.title: Watch as Celtic beat Rangers in Women's Scottish Cup final
articles.summary: Watch as a Morgan Cross goal is enough to give Celtic victory over Rangers in the Women's Scottish Cup final despite a red card for Emma Lawton.

articles.title: Introducing Shorts on the BBC Sport app
articles.summary: From stunning goals and shocking interviews to unforgettable celebrations, some sporting moments are made to be watched, not just read about - and that's why BBC Sport are launching Shorts.

articles.title: Fenerbahce presidential rivals both want Greenwood
articles.summary: Former England and Manchester United striker Mason Greenwood is a key transfer target for both Fenerbahce's presidential candidates

articles.title: Keegan reveals stage four cancer diagnosis
articles.summary: Former England captain and manager Kevin Keegan reveals he has stage four cancer.

articles.title: Record-holder Milner retires after 24-year career
articles.summary: Former England international James Milner announces his retirement after a 24-year Premier League career.

articles.title: The football fans who went to a World Cup and loved it so much, they stayed
articles.summary: A new documentary follows a group of Wolves fans who moved to the US after the Mexico World Cup.

articles.title: Wolves rejection to double Champions League winner: Vitinha's rise to top
articles.summary: BBC Sport NI charts Vitinha's rise from an unsuccessful loan spell at Wolves to helping PSG win the Champions League twice in a row.

articles.title: How Slot went from champion to sack in a year
articles.summary: Just one year after lifting Liverpool's 20th league title in his debut season, Arne Slot has been sacked. BBC Sport looks back on how a dream start unravelled on Merseyside.

articles.title: Southampton owner will not sack Eckert over Spygate
articles.summary: Southampton owner Dragan Solak tells BBC Sport he will not sack Tonda Eckert, despite the "mistake" the head coach made when authorising a spying campaign against rival Championship clubs.

articles.title: 'You legend. Manager loved it' - Southampton's Spygate WhatsApps revealed
articles.summary: New WhatsApp messages have revealed how Southampton orchestrated a spying campaign against their Championship rivals.

articles.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
articles.summary: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.
